user_query:
I’m organizing hematology study materials for a study group. Please turn my notes and study log into structured files, a progress summary, and meeting notes with action items.

Inputs under the workspace:
- input/hematology_notes.md: Study notes using tagged lines. Tags include: Topic:, Term:, Definition:, Mnemonic:, Links:, Q:, TODO:. Links are comma-separated keywords.
- input/study_log.csv: My recent study log with headers: date,topic,hours,comprehension_rating,outstanding_tasks.
- input/meeting_context.txt: Meeting info containing a title, date/time, attendees, and goal.

Deliverables (save exactly to these paths):
1) output/concepts.json: Structured extraction of all terms from input/hematology_notes.md. Use this JSON shape:
   { "terms": [ { "topic": string, "term": string, "definition": string, "mnemonic": string|null, "links": [string] } ] }
   Requirements:
   - Create one object per line starting with "Term:". The object’s fields must be sourced from the nearest preceding "Topic:" and the subsequent tagged lines for that term. If "Mnemonic:" is absent for a term, set it to null.
   - For "Links:", split by commas and trim whitespace into an array. If a term has no Links: line, use an empty array.
   - The number of objects in terms must equal the number of "Term:" lines in input/hematology_notes.md.

2) output/questions_todos.json: Extract open questions and tasks from input/hematology_notes.md using this JSON shape:
   { "questions": [string], "todos": [string] }
   Requirements:
   - Include all lines starting with "Q:" in questions (strip the leading tag).
   - Include all lines starting with "TODO:" in todos (strip the leading tag).
   - Preserve the original wording (minus the tag) so they can be cross-checked against the input file.

3) output/progress_report.md: A concise status update based on input/study_log.csv. Include the following sections exactly: "Summary", "Metrics", "Recently Completed", "Gaps and Next Focus".
   Requirements:
   - Metrics: total hours studied (sum of hours), overall average comprehension_rating (to one decimal), list of unique topics studied, and count of log entries.
   - Recently Completed: the 3 most recent rows by date with topic, hours, comprehension_rating.
   - Gaps and Next Focus: list topics that either have an average comprehension_rating < 3 or have any non-empty outstanding_tasks. Briefly note which condition triggered the gap for each topic.
   - Base all calculations strictly on input/study_log.csv.

4) output/meeting_notes.md: Prepare meeting notes for the upcoming study group based on input/meeting_context.txt, output/questions_todos.json, and findings in output/progress_report.md.
   Requirements:
   - Include sections: "Meeting Info" (title, date/time, attendees), "Agenda" (derived from questions in output/questions_todos.json and gaps from the progress report), and "Action Items".
   - Action Items: at least 5 items. Each must have Description, Origin, Owner, Due Date.
     • Description: a concrete task.
     • Origin: cite the exact source text from either a questions_todos entry (question or todo) or an outstanding_tasks value from input/study_log.csv.
     • Owner: choose Self (Alex) or Peer (Sam) according to meeting_context.
     • Due Date: set to meeting date + 14 days (YYYY-MM-DD) based strictly on the meeting date in input/meeting_context.txt.
   - Ensure each action item’s Origin matches verbatim text from one of the inputs (minus tags for notes), so it can be verified.

Notes:
- Use only the provided inputs; do not fetch anything from the internet.
- Write all outputs under the output/ directory as specified, overwriting any existing files.
- Make sure every required operation is essential to completing this request: create structured files (JSON), compose the progress report, and prepare meeting notes with action items.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
